test.name: Extract assertions from Edward Becker's 1914 Kentucky marriage
test.description: 1914 Kentucky marriage of Edward Becker and Ella May Horne ('Kentucky, Marriages, 1785-1979', ark:/61903/1:1:FWTT-8RH). Real record from the Ferber/Becker research (#799). Names both parties' parents; Edward's mother is recorded only as 'Anna' - part of the Anna/Mary Kramer name conflict. Edward's stated birth (~1887) differs from the census Edward (~1876/77).

input.user_message:
Extract assertions from this marriage record:

Kentucky, Marriages, 1785-1979
ark:/61903/1:1:FWTT-8RH

Marriage date: 30 June 1914
Marriage place: Kentucky

Groom: Edward Becker
  Marital status: Single
  Birth: about 1887, Cincinnati, Ohio
  Father: John Becker
  Mother: Anna (no surname stated)

Bride: Ella May Horne
  Marital status: Single
  Birth: about 1890, Lexington, Kentucky
  Father: Harry Horne
  Mother: Carrie (no surname stated)

judge_context: Marriage date (30 Jun 1914) and place (Kentucky) are DIRECT evidence - explicitly stated
Extract assertions for BOTH parties - Edward Becker and Ella May Horne
Parents named by the parties follow the marriage-record doctrine (ut_record_extraction_010): DIRECT. Edward's parents = John Becker + Anna; Ella May's = Harry Horne + Carrie
Edward's mother is recorded only as 'Anna' with no surname - extract exactly as stated; do NOT supply 'Kramer' or reconcile with other records (that is downstream)

Edward's stated birth (~1887) differs from the census Edward (~1876/1877) - extract the birth as stated on THIS record; the discrepancy is a downstream identity/conflict question, not an extraction fix
Birth entries give a place and an approximate year - extract as stated; do NOT invent an exact date
Informant: each party is the informant for their own facts (proximity 'self'); the clerk is the recorder
Source classification is DERIVATIVE - a FamilySearch index of the Kentucky marriage record
